flashcards.definition: A key chemistry concept about metallic bonds.
Describes metallic bonding: metal cations are surrounded by delocalized valence electrons free to move throughout the structure.
The ability of a metal to be hammered into sheets; due to non-directional metallic bonds allowing layers to slide.
A mixture of two or more metals (or a metal and a nonmetal) with properties different from the pure components. Examples: steel (Fe+C), brass (Cu+Zn).
Metals conduct electricity and heat well because delocalized electrons are free to move and transfer energy throughout the metallic lattice.
